zh.lifeSummary: 當Blanche de France於1253年出生在以色列מחוז תל אביביפו時，她的父親Rey Luis IX de Francia Santo Canonizado de la Iglesia Católica.39歲，母親Margarita de Provenza Reina Consorte de Francia32歲。她於1269年11月30日與Fernando de la Cerda "Infante de Castilla"在西班牙迪奧希斯 德 布爾戈斯結婚。他們至少是2個兒子的父母。她去世於1320年6月7日在法國i萊-德-福朗斯芭黎絲享年67歲，埋葬在法國i萊-德-福朗斯芭黎絲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Blanche de France于1253年出生在以色列מחוז תל אביביפו时，她的父亲Rey Luis IX de Francia Santo Canonizado de la Iglesia Católica.39岁，母亲Margarita de Provenza Reina Consorte de Francia32岁。她于1269年11月30日与Fernando de la Cerda "Infante de Castilla"在西班牙迪奧希斯 德 布爾戈斯结婚。他们至少是2个儿子的父母。她去世于1320年6月7日在法国i萊-德-福朗斯芭黎絲享年67岁，埋葬在法国i萊-德-福朗斯芭黎絲。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: France
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: ethnic name for someone from France (see 2 below).

French: habitational name given to someone from France (at the time when the name only applied to those lands belonging to the French king, covering only a small part of its current territory) or from Île-de-France (i.e. from a region centered on Paris); or, in southern France, an ethnic name for someone from what is now the northern half of the country, where the langue d'oïl (Old French as distinct from Occitan) was spoken. Compare   Defrance   and   Lafrance  .
Czech (Franče): from a pet form of František, Latin Franciscus (see Francis ).

variants.name: Defrance
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Emile, Henri, Jean-Paul.

French (mainly northern): habitational name, with fused preposition de ‘from’, given to someone from France (at the time when the name only applied to those lands belonging to the French king, covering only a small part of its current territory) or from Île-de-France (i.e. from a region centered on Paris). Compare France .

nameMeanings.name: Blanche
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a nickname for a blonde, from blanche, feminine of Old French blanc ‘white’ (of Germanic origin). It came to be associated with the notion of whiteness as indicating purity, and was introduced into England as a given name by the Normans. A pale complexion combined with light hair was long an ideal of beauty in Europe (compare Modern English fair, which at first meant ‘beautiful’ and then, from the 16th century, ‘light in colouring’).

en.lifeSummary: When Blanche de France was born in 1253, in Jaffa, Tel Aviv District, Israel, her father, Rey Luis IX de Francia Santo Canonizado de la Iglesia Católica., was 39 and her mother, Margarita de Provenza Reina Consorte de Francia, was 32. She married Fernando de la Cerda "Infante de Castilla" on 30 November 1269, in Diócesis de Burgos, Spain. They were the parents of at least 2 sons. She died on 7 June 1320, in Paris, Île-de-France, France, at the age of 67, and was buried in Paris, Île-de-France, France.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Blanche de France est née en 1253, à Yafo, Tel Aviv District, Israël, son père, Rey Luis IX de Francia Santo Canonizado de la Iglesia Católica., avait 39 ans et sa mère, Margarita de Provenza Reina Consorte de Francia, avait 32 ans. Elle a épousé Fernando de la Cerda "Infante de Castilla" le 30 novembre 1269, à Diócesis de Burgos, Espagne. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils. Elle est décédée le 7 juin 1320, à Paris, Île-de-France, France, à l’âge de 67 ans, et a été enterrée à Paris, Île-de-France, France.

de.lifeSummary: Als Blanche de France 1253 in Yafo, Tel Aviv District, Israel geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Rey Luis IX de Francia Santo Canonizado de la Iglesia Católica. 39 und ihre Mutter Margarita de Provenza Reina Consorte de Francia 32. Sie hat am 30. November 1269 in Diócesis de Burgos, Spanien Fernando de la Cerda "Infante de Castilla" geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne. Sie starb am 7. Juni 1320 im Alter von 67 Jahren in Paris, Île-de-France, Frankreich und wurde in Paris, Île-de-France, Frankreich begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Blanche de France è nata il nel 1253 nel Yafo, Tel Aviv District, Israele, sua padre, Rey Luis IX de Francia Santo Canonizado de la Iglesia Católica., aveva 39 anni e sua madre, Margarita de Provenza Reina Consorte de Francia, aveva 32 anni. Lei sposato Fernando de la Cerda "Infante de Castilla" il 30 novembre 1269, nel Diócesis de Burgos, Spagna. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli. Morì il 7 giugno 1320, nel Paris, Île-de-France, Francia, all'età di 67 anni, e fu sepolta a Paris, Île-de-France, Francia.

ko.lifeSummary: Blanche de France(이)가 1253년 이스라엘 מחוז תל אביב יפו에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Rey Luis IX de Francia Santo Canonizado de la Iglesia Católica.(은)는 39세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Margarita de Provenza Reina Consorte de Francia(은)는 32세였다. 그녀는 1269년 11월 30일 스페인 디o스시스 데 버고스에서 Fernando de la Cerda "Infante de Castilla"(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1320년 6월 7일 프랑스 i레-데-프랜스 파리스에서 67세의 나이로 사망했으며 프랑스 i레-데-프랜스 파리스에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Blanche de France родилась в 1253 г. в местности יפו, מחוז תל אביב, Израиль, её отцу по имени Rey Luis IX de Francia Santo Canonizado de la Iglesia Católica. было 39 лет, а матери, которую звали Margarita de Provenza Reina Consorte de Francia, 32. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Fernando de la Cerda "Infante de Castilla" 30 ноября 1269 г. в местности диoсесис де бургос, Испания. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына. Она умерла 7 июня 1320 г. в местности пэрис, iле-де-фрэнс, Франция в возрасте 67 лет и была похоронена в местности пэрис, iле-де-фрэнс, Франция.

Se casó con Fernando de la Cerda "Infante de Castilla" el 30 de noviembre de 1269, en Diócesis de Burgos, España. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos. Murió el 7 de junio de 1320, en Paris, Île-de-France, Francia, a la edad de 67 años, y fue sepultada en Paris, Île-de-France, Francia.
